food_name: Bun Rieu (Vietnamese Tomato and Crab Paste Noodle Soup)
region_of_origin: Vietnam — Northern Vietnamese (Hanoi-origin); popular throughout Vietnam
food_subgroup: Vietnamese noodle soup — rice vermicelli in tomato-crab paste broth with tofu, pork, and shrimp

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No specific FoodOn URI exists for Bun Rieu. Closest parent would be a generic noodle soup concept. Null retained pending FoodOn expansion for Southeast Asian dishes.
ontology.foodex2_description: Vietnamese crab paste noodle soup (Bun Rieu) — closest parent match in FoodEx2 hierarchy for Southeast Asian noodle soups
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: A0CMF is the closest defensible FoodEx2 code for a Vietnamese noodle soup dish; no exact code exists for Bun Rieu specifically.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code exists for Bun Rieu as a specific food concept.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard bowl of Bun Rieu weighs approximately 500g, comprising roughly 280g broth, 120g rice vermicelli noodles, and 100g toppings including crab paste dumpling, deep-fried tofu, and pork.

nutrient_source.title: FAO/INFOODS Food Composition Table for Southeast Asia (2020) — Bun Rieu component calculation; supplemented by USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for individual ingredients
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values derived from FAO/INFOODS SEA 2020 table combined with USDA SR Legacy component-level calculation for Bun Rieu. No single-entry exact match exists for this dish in either database; values represent a weighted component calculation based on standard recipe proportions (rice vermicelli, tomato broth, crab paste, fermented shrimp paste, tofu, pork, shrimp). Confidence: moderate (0.7).

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values — rice vermicelli (bún) as analogous food; Atkinson et al. 2008, Diabetes Care
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Bun Rieu as a complete dish. GI ~58 is estimated from rice vermicelli (bún), the primary carbohydrate source, which has a published GI of approximately 58 (medium) per University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care 31(12):2281-2283). GL per 500g bowl calculated as: (GI × available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (58 × 50) / 100 = 29.0. The tomato-based broth and protein components (crab paste, shrimp, tofu) may modestly lower postprandial glucose response relative to plain rice vermicelli.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium ~1,200mg per bowl (240mg/100g) — primarily from fermented shrimp paste (mắm tôm) and crab paste broth
hypertension.guidance: At ~1,200mg sodium per bowl (approximately 60% of the 2,000mg daily limit recommended for hypertensive patients), Bun Rieu poses a high sodium concern; patients should request reduced mắm tôm in the broth and avoid adding extra fermented shrimp paste at the table.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: GI ~58 (medium), GL ~29.0 (high) per bowl — driven by 50g available carbohydrate from rice vermicelli (bún)
type2_diabetes.guidance: The glycemic load of ~29.0 per standard bowl is high for T2DM management; patients should request a reduced noodle portion (half serving of bún) and rely on the protein-rich toppings (crab paste, shrimp, tofu) to moderate postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High sodium (~1,200mg/bowl) is the primary CVD concern; saturated fat is moderate at 0.8g/100g (4g per bowl); tomato lycopene (~7–12mg per bowl) provides antioxidant benefit
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For cardiovascular disease patients, the primary concern is the high sodium load from fermented shrimp paste; the dish's tomato lycopene content (~7–12mg per bowl after cooking) offers Grade C antioxidant benefit, but sodium reduction through smaller mắm tôm portions is the priority modification.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Tomato lycopene (7–12mg per bowl) and soy isoflavones from tofu provide anti-inflammatory benefit; high sodium (~1,200mg) is a concern for patients on corticosteroids
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Bun Rieu provides clinically relevant lycopene (~7–12mg per bowl, Grade C anti-inflammatory evidence) and soy isoflavones from tofu; however, the high sodium content (~1,200mg per bowl) warrants caution for rheumatoid arthritis patients on corticosteroid therapy due to fluid retention risk.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Bun Rieu (bún riêu) is a Northern Vietnamese noodle soup featuring rice vermicelli (bún) in a deeply flavoured red-orange broth built from ripe tomatoes, fermented shrimp paste (mắm tôm), and concentrated freshwater crab paste (gạch cua). The dish is topped with crab paste dumplings, deep-fried tofu, sliced pork, and shrimp, producing a complex umami-rich, slightly tangy, and pungent flavour profile. The broth colour comes from the tomatoes and crab roe, giving it a distinctive appearance compared to the clear broths of pho or the lemongrass-spiced broth of bún bò Huế. Per standard bowl (~500g with broth): approximately 410 kcal, 22g protein, 12g fat, 50g carbohydrate, GI ~58 (medium), GL ~29.0 (high), and ~1,200mg sodium. The fermented shrimp paste (mắm tôm) is the primary sodium driver, contributing approximately 500mg sodium per bowl at typical usage of 10g. Clinically, the tomatoes provide lycopene (~7–12mg per bowl after cooking), a dose considered clinically relevant for antioxidant and anti-inflammatory benefit (Grade C evidence for CRP reduction). The tofu contributes soy isoflavones with mild anti-inflammatory properties. The high glycemic load (GL ~29.0) makes portion control of the rice vermicelli important for T2DM patients. Bun Rieu is a Hanoi-origin dish widely available throughout Vietnam and at Vietnamese restaurants in Singapore, though less common than pho or bún bò Huế. The dish is naturally gluten-free and dairy-free. Source: FAO/INFOODS SEA 2020 + USDA SR Legacy component calculation. Confidence: moderate.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values derived from FAO/INFOODS SEA 2020 combined with USDA SR Legacy component-level calculation; no single approved-source entry exists for Bun Rieu as a complete dish. GI estimated from rice vermicelli analogue (University of Sydney GI Database). Overall confidence is moderate (0.7) reflecting the component-calculation methodology and absence of a direct dish-level measurement in any approved primary source.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractionation data for Bun Rieu as a composite dish are not available in FAO/INFOODS SEA 2020 or USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy as of 2026. water_g set to -1: moisture content for the complete dish (including broth) is not reported as a single value in the approved sources consulted. trans_fat_g is reported as 0.0 (confirmed negligible/trace per USDA SR Legacy component data for the constituent ingredients). All other per_100g values are supported by FAO/INFOODS SEA 2020 + USDA SR Legacy component calculation. GI ~58 is an analogous estimate based on rice vermicelli (bún) as the primary carbohydrate source per University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care). GL per bowl = (58 × 50g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 29.0. Lycopene content (~7–12mg per bowl after cooking) is noted in clinical context but not included as a per_100g field as it is not a standard RESULT template field. The dish is not reliably halal-certified due to the use of fermented shrimp paste (mắm tôm) which may contain trace alcohol from fermentation; halal flag set to false pending certification confirmation.

Added Chinese simplified (越南番茄蟹膏汤粉) and traditional (越南番茄蟹膏湯粉) forms to local_names.
Expanded aliases flat array to include Vietnamese native-script forms, Chinese script forms, romanized Chinese, and regional variants (bún riêu cua).
Populated aliases_by_language with consistent entries verified against flat aliases array.
Converted health_suitability conditions to health_context format with human_condition_name, concern_level, key_factor, and clinical guidance.
Renamed 'diabetes' condition to type2_diabetes with human_condition_name 'Type 2 Diabetes' per scoring model requirement.
Added Cardiovascular Disease health context (required by Q7 but absent in input).
Documented -1 fields (monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, water_g) in notes with source consultation rationale.
Expanded gi_source to reference University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 peer-reviewed journal.
Expanded natural_language_description to exceed 200 characters with full clinical and cultural context.
Added ontology gap notes for FoodOn and SNOMED CT null values.
Set confidence to 'medium' consistent with 0.7 input confidence and component-calculation methodology.
